children.tooltip: Search for users by name to start a conversation
children.label: Search

children.onItemClick: {{(async function() { const msg = List_Messages.triggeredItem; if (Number(msg.senderId) === Number(appsmith.store.userId)) { await storeValue("editingMessageId", msg.id); await storeValue("editingContent", msg.content); resetWidget("Input2"); showAlert("Editing message. Modify text below and tap send to update.", "info"); } })()}}

children.placeholderText: {{appsmith.store.editingMessageId ? "Editing message — modify and send to update..." : (appsmith.store.selectedUser?.userId ? "Type a message..." : "Select a contact first to start chatting")}}

children.onClick: {{(async function() { if (appsmith.store.editingMessageId) { await storeValue('editingContent', Input2.text); await Update_Message.run(); showAlert('Message updated!', 'success'); await storeValue('editingMessageId', null); await storeValue('editingContent', ''); resetWidget('Input2'); await JSObject5.loadConversation(); } else { await JSObject4.sendMessage(); } })()}}

children.text: Are you sure you want to delete this chat?

children.tooltip: Save this route to your profile for quick access later
Select a previously saved route to load its start and end addresses
children.onOptionChange: {{(function(){ const route = GetSavedRoutes.data?.routes?.find(r => String(r.id) === SelectSavedRoute.selectedOptionValue); if(route){ StartAddress.setValue(route.startAddress); EndAddress.setValue(route.endAddress); } })()}}

children.onClick: {{(function(){ const id = SelectSavedRoute.selectedOptionValue; if(!id){showAlert('Select a route first','warning');return;} DeleteSavedRoute.run({routeId: id}).then(()=>{showAlert('Route deleted','success');GetSavedRoutes.run();}).catch(()=>showAlert('Failed to delete','error')); })()}}

{{(async function(){ const id = SelectSavedRoute.selectedOptionValue; if(!id){showAlert('Select a route first','warning');return;} const route = GetSavedRoutes.data?.routes?.find(r => String(r.id) === id); const newName = (StartAddress.text && EndAddress.text) ? StartAddress.text + ' → ' + EndAddress.text : (route?.name || 'Renamed Route'); await storeValue('editingRouteId', id); await storeValue('editingRouteName', newName); await UpdateSavedRoute.run(); showAlert('Route renamed to: ' + newName, 'success'); await storeValue('editingRouteId', null); await storeValue('editingRouteName', ''); await GetSavedRoutes.run(); })()}}

children.tooltip: Preference for routes near hospitals/clinics? (0=no preference, 10=stay very close to health services)

children.text:
Our Mission Make Toronto’s streets accessible, safe, and navigable for everyone.
Most navigation apps, such as Google Maps and Apple Maps, optimize for distance or travel time alone. They do not consider wheelchair accessibility, pedestrian safety, active construction, or proximity to essential services. NavSafe changes that. How It Works NavSafe uses a custom A* pathfinding algorithm that weighs real Toronto city data, including crime rates, street lighting density, wheelchair accessibility (curb cuts and surface quality), active construction zones, and hospital proximity, to calculate personalized, context-aware walking routes. Each user sets their own preference weights (on a 0–10 scale) so the route is tailored to their specific needs and comfort level. Technology The backend is built with Java 17 and Spring Boot 3.2, using Spring Data JPA with an H2 embedded database. The routing engine loads the road network from OpenStreetMap via the Overpass API and builds an in-memory graph with a grid-based spatial index. The frontend is a mobile-first Appsmith application with Leaflet.js for interactive map rendering. Everything is containerized with Docker for one-command deployment.

Powered by real city data, users get a personalized route based on what matters to them.
  • Hospital proximity: Stay within a set distance of the nearest health service at every point of your walk.
  • Street lighting: Prefer well-lit streets, especially for nighttime routes.
  • Wheelchair accessibility: We check curb cuts, ramps, and surface quality from OpenStreetMap tags.
  • Construction avoidance: Route around active City of Toronto road reconstruction projects.
  • Crime awareness: Factor in neighbourhood crime density so you can choose safer streets.
We’ve got you covered.
The Team We are a team of eight student developers from the University of Toronto, building NavSafe for MIE350 (Winter 2025).
Arshia FeizmohammadyBackend Lead, Algorithm Design
Thomas (Jigao) WangBackend & Integration Co-Lead
Andrew YinBackend, Algorithm Design
Simon ZhuBackend, Algorithm
Hamad AlmheiriFrontend Lead
Dooyeon (Dunny) YeomFrontend, Project Manager
Ivan MusubaFrontend
Maria ChzhenBackend & Integration Co-Lead, Frontend, Algorithm Design
Data Sources & References
  • Major Crime Indicators: Toronto Police Service via Toronto Open Data Portal (2014–2024). Used for neighbourhood-level crime density scoring.
  • Poles (Street Lighting): City of Toronto Open Data. Filtered to subtype 6006 (street light poles) for lighting coverage analysis.
  • Road Reconstruction Program: City of Toronto Open Data. Active projects used to identify construction zones.
  • Health Services: City of Toronto Open Data. Locations of hospitals and health agencies for proximity calculations.
  • Cycling Network: City of Toronto Open Data. Infrastructure type, surface, and geometry for multi-modal awareness.
  • OpenStreetMap: Road network graph with surface type, wheelchair, and lit tags. Downloaded via the Overpass API.
  • Nominatim: OpenStreetMap geocoding service for address-to-coordinate conversion.
  • Google Maps / Apple Maps — Conventional shortest-path navigation tools used as a baseline comparison.
Disclaimer This is an educational project developed for MIE350 at the University of Toronto. Route suggestions are for demonstration purposes only and should not be relied upon for safety-critical navigation decisions. The underlying datasets may not be current or complete, and the crime, lighting, and construction data is sourced from publicly available City of Toronto records that may contain gaps or delays. Always exercise personal judgment when navigating.

actionConfiguration.body:
{{
{
  userId: appsmith.store.userId,
  name: StartAddress.text + ' → ' + EndAddress.text,
  startAddress: StartAddress.text,
  endAddress: EndAddress.text,
  startLat: GeocodeStart.data?.lat,
  startLng: GeocodeStart.data?.lng,
  endLat: GeocodeEnd.data?.lat,
  endLng: GeocodeEnd.data?.lng,
  wheelchairWeight: Get_User_Preferences.data?.preferences?.wheelchairWeight || 0,
  crimeWeight: Get_User_Preferences.data?.preferences?.crimeWeight || 0,
  lightingWeight: Get_User_Preferences.data?.preferences?.lightingWeight || 0,
  constructionWeight: Get_User_Preferences.data?.preferences?.constructionWeight || 0,
  timeOfDay: Get_User_Preferences.data?.preferences?.timeOfDay || 12,
  maxDistanceToHospital: Get_User_Preferences.data?.preferences?.maxDistanceToHospital || 0
}
}}
